summary: Defer WIF adoption until the coordinated credential-rotation window; use it as the preferred research path for replacing long-lived OpenAI API keys if supported.

community_evidence.production_deployments: Unknown from this tick unless noted; source evidence was official changelog/docs or GitHub metadata.
community_evidence.practitioner_sentiment: Limited scan only; no broad Reddit/YouTube validation completed in this short pump tick.

evaluation.what_it_makes_obsolete: None immediately; may reduce reliance on long-lived secrets or inform future agent infrastructure.
evaluation.cost_of_switching: Non-trivial; must be tested in clone/rotation context before production use.
evaluation.cost_of_staying: Continue current known constraints; no immediate regression.

rationale: [verified] OpenAI says WIF exchanges externally issued workload identity tokens for short-lived OpenAI access tokens without storing long-lived API keys. This directly maps to BACKLOG-006, but [unverified] provider-stack support for CTO/OpenClaw/Codex OAuth is unknown and cannot be changed safely outside the coordinated credential rotation window.

test_results.notes: Research-only decision; no install, spend, external mutation, or credential change performed.

rollback.instructions: No runtime change to roll back.
